title: sambucus racemosa var. glabra
intro: Sambucus racemosa var. glabra, also known as the American red elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to western North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa var. glabra include potential relief from cold and flu symptoms, as well as support for the immune system and cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's berries, leaves, and flowers are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being.
